textDisplay: Ok, I don’t know about this 🤷🏻‍♂️, but I think this movie and the death note remake Netflix made are in the same universe 🌎. Well, let’s see, Detective Elliot 🕵🏾‍♂️ (Andre in Get out, and L in Death Note) is a detective, and he is the same actor as L in the death note movie. And the writer 🖊 and creator of the DEATH NOTE anime and manga, confirmed that L’s 🙎🏻‍♂️ last name was Elliot. And well, this guy is a detective, same actor as in Death note remake, and his last name is Elliot. 😑😐🙄 I know how this sounds, but I’d be cool, I guess, maybe L, as a detective has to change roles and show his face to make him be less suspicious, maybe creating identities so L is harder to find out who he is. I don’t know 🤦🏼‍♂️🤦🏻‍♂️🤦🏻‍♀️🤦🏾‍♀️, but I think it may be true, and until this movie comes out, I’m hope it is (For some weird reason).

textDisplay: I saw this at the Toronto International Film Festival and it was great! It's so much fun and very funny. The audience I saw it with was having a great time watching it. It was one of the hottest tickets to get at the festival. Daniel Craig, Chris Evans, Ana De Armas, Jamie Lee Curtis, Toni Collette were all great and the standouts in the cast. Can't wait to see it again. Don't dismiss it just because Rian Johnson wrote and directed it, it's a really fun film.
textOriginal: I saw this at the Toronto International Film Festival and it was great! It's so much fun and very funny. The audience I saw it with was having a great time watching it. It was one of the hottest tickets to get at the festival. Daniel Craig, Chris Evans, Ana De Armas, Jamie Lee Curtis, Toni Collette were all great and the standouts in the cast. Can't wait to see it again. Don't dismiss it just because Rian Johnson wrote and directed it, it's a really fun film.

textDisplay:
I actually spent five minutes scrolling through these comments, and I can NOT believe how many people are either commenting based on their hate of Last Jedi and how apparently Brick and Looper weren't good movies, OR non-fucking-stop about Chris Evans, and Captain America. This one is even MORE worth noting since the reason he took on this role, playing a character who is a complete departure from Cap, is exactly that-


He wants to do other things, and the comments about him cursing demonstrate how far that has gone so far. He's great as Cap, no question there- but I wish him luck in other roles and if anything is VERY clear from even just this trailer- he's having a blast playing against typecast, same as Craig is clearly enjoying over-embellishing that ridiculously southern sounding accent(though if you want to see more of this from Craig, check out Lucky Logan).


As for people being reminded of Murder on the Orient Express- Agatha Christie wrote that one, and many other murder mystery novels, such as "And Then There Were None", "The A.B.C. Murders", and so on(she also created Hercule Poirot who, if you don't know him, is a detective as famous as Sherlock Holmes.) - and this movie is VERY much inspired by her work, and the work of other mystery novel authors from the past...hell the title font/style being used for this trailer and the posters, is exactly like the font and text style that would be used on those very novels.


You have an incredibly talented cast here, an attempt to bring a classic genre into modern times, and if the trailer is any indication, some seriously impressive lighting and camera work.

As for people being reminded of Murder on the Orient Express- Agatha Christie wrote that one, and many other murder mystery novels, such as "And Then There Were None", "The A.B.C. Murders", and so on(she also created Hercule Poirot who, if you don't know him, is a detective as famous as Sherlock Holmes.) - and this movie is VERY much inspired by her work, and the work of other mystery novel authors from the past...hell the title font/style being used for this trailer and the posters, is exactly like the font and text style that would be used on those very novels.


You have an incredibly talented cast here, an attempt to bring a classic genre into modern times, and if the trailer is any indication, some seriously impressive lighting and camera work.

textDisplay: I have hope, but I'm cautious. Rian Johnson is a great filmmaker, but a messy script writer (Last Jedi is proof of that). HOWEVER, maybe The Last Jedi script was rushed, and he was able to take his time and get the Knives Out script really tight and clean, instead of writing it in one messy draft like he did with Last Jedi's script. So I'm willing to give this a chance, especially since I love mystery whodunnit movies
textOriginal: I have hope, but I'm cautious. Rian Johnson is a great filmmaker, but a messy script writer (Last Jedi is proof of that). HOWEVER, maybe The Last Jedi script was rushed, and he was able to take his time and get the Knives Out script really tight and clean, instead of writing it in one messy draft like he did with Last Jedi's script. So I'm willing to give this a chance, especially since I love mystery whodunnit movies

textDisplay: I'm not the biggest fan of TLJ but goddamnit this isn't fucking Star Wars guys. This isn't a film that already has Story arc's woven into it that he can easily ruin & mess up in order to make the film HIS OWN. Regardless of what he did with TLJ, The man is still a good writer & director. If anything, TLJ is evidence that he's not good at taken ALREADY WRITTEN story arc's & making them is own. He's at his best when he's dealing with his own material & not someone else's LIKE TLJ.

textDisplay: @Danbo22987 hell yeah I saw looper. And i very much enjoyed that movie too. That's why I was hyped for the last jedi. But the bigger problem with Ryan (other than making a movie myself and many others didn't enjoy) was how he blamed the fanbase for not liking his film. That to me was very revealing about the kind of guy he is. And the fans acted according by saying f u and boycotting Solo (a mediocre, but decent enough star wars movie). So while I'm not doubting the quality of his work on future releases, it's his name alone that makes me say pass. The door is always open for him to redeem himself.

textDisplay: Ugh 😑 this looks like a disjointed, ill-conceived mess; he’s just remaking CLUE. With all the Rian Johnson branding placed in the 4 corners of this trailer, Lionsgate is telling us exactly who to blame when we walk out of this film in disgust. & what’s with Chris Evans’ gross dialogue? “Eat shit, eat shit, eat shit”??? 🤷🏼‍♀️😳 I think Johnson is surreptitiously using Evans as his proxy to curse out the fans & critics who’ve righteously dissed his work. & isn’t Johnson a USC film school brat? textDisplay: Okay...I was looking forward to this (though, his crimes against the Star Wars Sequel Trilogy will forever remain legendary), but, it seems that this film may be trying to do the same thing that TLJ did, by circumventing the concepts of the classic "whodunit", which isn't necessarily a bad thing...BUT, after witnessing The Last Jedi, I'm very wary of ghouls dressed up to look like plush animals. I would see it for the cast, not really for who created it. I think I'm going to need to hear what critics and general audiences think, before committing to witness this on the big screen.
textOriginal: Okay...I was looking forward to this (though, his crimes against the Star Wars Sequel Trilogy will forever remain legendary), but, it seems that this film may be trying to do the same thing that TLJ did, by circumventing the concepts of the classic "whodunit", which isn't necessarily a bad thing...BUT, after witnessing The Last Jedi, I'm very wary of ghouls dressed up to look like plush animals. I would see it for the cast, not really for who created it. I think I'm going to need to hear what critics and general audiences think, before committing to witness this on the big screen.

textDisplay: I like Rian Johnson and I like the cast, but to be honest this trailer kind of put me off of the movie a little bit. The trailer makes it look a bit too cheesy/whimsical for my tastes. Though it's perfectly possible that this is just a bad trailer. Another compliant I have is that the cinematography looks a little plain (not bad, just very uninteresting). Even if the film is in in a different sub-genre, I felt the way that Johnson shot some of his other films like Brick and Looper gave them much more personality and atmosphere.
textOriginal: I like Rian Johnson and I like the cast, but to be honest this trailer kind of put me off of the movie a little bit. The trailer makes it look a bit too cheesy/whimsical for my tastes. Though it's perfectly possible that this is just a bad trailer. Another compliant I have is that the cinematography looks a little plain (not bad, just very uninteresting). Even if the film is in in a different sub-genre, I felt the way that Johnson shot some of his other films like Brick and Looper gave them much more personality and atmosphere.
